note: Deterministic output-guard signatures (Layer 1) for the drafted reply. Edit this file to add, tune, or remove signatures WITHOUT a code change: output.py loads and compiles it at startup. Each family has a snake_case `category` (the label surfaced on a flag), a human `description`, a list of `patterns` (case-insensitive Python regular expressions), and an optional `redact` flag. When `redact` is true the matched snippet is MASKED to its last four digits before it is recorded as evidence, so a flag never carries a raw account/card number into the verdict, logs, or audit trail (SPEC §5/§7). Remember JSON escaping: a regex backslash is written as `\` here (so `\s+` is the regex `\s+`). Any change here is a safety control: add a test in test_guard_output.py and get it reviewed.

families.description: Financial or legal commitments the assistant must not make without a human: guarantees, personal promises, unconditional refund/reimbursement commitments, definite assurances, and accepting liability (SPEC §4.6).

Sensitive personal identifiers leaking into the reply: full card/account numbers (13-19 digits, optionally grouped by spaces or dashes) and US Social Security numbers. Evidence is masked to the last four digits. A short 'ending in 1234' reference is only four digits and does not match.
